mainHtml:
Home/Solutions/3D Format Conversion

Let 3D Files Move Smoothly Across Systems

Support conversion between common 3D file formats so design, quotation, manufacturing and collaboration systems can keep using the same information.

Submit File

Solve Model Opening Issues Across Software

Format conversion handles common STEP, IGES, STL, OBJ and 3MF file transfer issues while checking whether units, mesh integrity and assembly hierarchy affect later quotation.

For files that need machining, printing or engineering review, the page keeps before-and-after formats, versions and exception notes for later tracking.

STEPSTLIGESFormat Check

Recommended Preparation

  • Original 3D file
  • Target format
  • Software or equipment requirements
  • Whether to keep assembly hierarchy

Still Manufacturable After Conversion

Opening the file is only the first step. More importantly, later systems must read it, engineers must review it and manufacturing must keep using it.

Format OutputOutput a target format suitable for quotation, machining or printing workflows.
Basic CheckCheck whether the file has missing faces, abnormal units or missing assembly information.
Collaboration RecordKeep file information before and after conversion for project tracking.

Where Converted Files Go

For cross-software collaboration, supplier file transfer, legacy data migration and pre-quotation file cleanup.

01Readability CheckConfirm files can be read by quotation, DFM or CAM workflows, and flag unit, missing-face or assembly-loss risks.
02Use MatchingChoose formats for machining, 3D printing, display preview or engineering archive instead of forcing one file to serve every use.
03Version TraceRecord original file, target file and conversion notes so information sources remain traceable.

Convert 3D Files into a Format for Continued Collaboration

Submit the original file and target use. The system checks the format first, then prompts whether engineering review is needed.

Submit File
